title: Seeds, Origins, Paths, Query Space, Budgets, and Stop Policy · Language Clinic: Resource Budgets
summary: A seed expands to subdomains, downloads, calendars, search results, and logout links while the crawler has no page, byte, time, or incident limit. Predict authorization, identity, scope, parsing, extraction, crawl state, resource use, privacy, accessibility, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the resource budgets increment in bounded crawl-scope policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal d…
objectives: Budget pages, depth, requests, redirects, bytes, decompressed bytes, time, concurrency, retries, disk, browser contexts, and report rows.
Express permitted paths, methods, query keys, values, pagination, fragments, and content types as testable admission rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-web-scraper-python cumulative authorized crawler and accessible site-audit portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python policy, URL, response, tree, frontier, record,
and report models with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Sockets, DNS, public or private
sites, credentials, host files, native HTTPX, Beautiful Soup, lxml, Scrapy, Playwright browsers,
accessibility claims, legal review, load, schedules, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlPolicy:
    owner_authorization_id: str
    allowed_origins: tuple[str, ...]
    maximum_pages: int = 240
    maximum_response_bytes: int = 2_000_000
    minimum_origin_spacing_seconds: float = 1.0


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class CrawlEvidence:
    revision: str
    policy_digest: str
    frontier_digest: str
    report_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Authorized Python crawler portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Worked focus: scraper-resource-budgets — Budget pages, depth, requests, redirects, bytes, decompressed bytes, time, concurrency, retries, disk, browser contexts, and report rows.
# Evidence: scraper-worked-scraper-crawl-scope-policy-3
# Competency: scraper-worked-scraper-crawl-scope-policy-3.
# Case 5798: a keyboard and screen-reader report review.
# Operating constraint: the target owner must be able to pause the crawl.
# Changed case: change malformed markup or parser choice and preserve extraction provenance.
def worked_scraper_crawl_scope_policy_3(admitted_origins: int = 1, admitted_paths: int = 8, page_budget: int = 240, byte_budget: int = 12000000, stop_paths: int = 5) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "authorizationworked3",
        "url5798",
        "response3dekrow",
        "treeworked35798",
        "frontier5798worked3",
        "report3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if admitted_origins != 1:
        return False, admitted_origins, "origin-scope"
    if admitted_paths < 1:
        return False, admitted_paths, "path-policy"
    if page_budget < 1 or byte_budget < 1024:
        return False, page_budget, "resource-budget"
    if stop_paths < 3:
        return False, stop_paths, "stop-policy"
    assert admitted_origins == 1 and stop_paths >= 3
    return True, page_budget, "bounded-crawl-scope"
